channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Neil I. Sidi, C.P.A.
item.description:
Neil I. Sidi, C.P.A. reported a state registry affecting 1950 records in New York City, NY.
📊 Records Affected: 1950 | 📍 Location: New York City, NY | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Greenhouse Apartments
item.description:
Greenhouse Apartments reported a state registry affecting 3473 records in Boston, MA.
📊 Records Affected: 3473 | 📍 Location: Boston, MA | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
item.description:
Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description:
In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description:
In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
item.description:
Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Clear Communication: The Missing Link in Cybersecurity Success
item.description:
When technical expertise meets clear communication, cybersecurity teams thrive. Learn how to foster trust and collaboration across diverse working groups.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
item.description:
Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description:
A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description:
A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: EU Sanctions Companies in China, Iran for Cyberattacks
item.description:
These rulings prohibit the entities from entering or doing business in the European Union.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description:
MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says at RSAC 2026 Conference.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian APT Exploits Zimbra Vulnerability Against Ukraine
item.description:
Insufficient sanitization of CSS content within HTML emails leads to inline script execution when the message is opened in a browser. The post Russian APT Exploits Zimbra Vulnerability Against Ukraine appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Ben Harris, From Unintentional Young Hacker to Intentional Adult CEO
item.description:
Harris is a hacker with a rebellious spirit and a willingness to break rules in the pursuit of his purpose – but without causing harm or damage. The post Hacker Conversations: Ben Harris, From Unintentional Young Hacker to Intentional Adult CEO appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Security Firm Aura Discloses Data Breach Impacting 900,000 Records
item.description:
The information was stolen from a marketing tool after an employee fell victim to a targeted phone phishing attack. The post Security Firm Aura Discloses Data Breach Impacting 900,000 Records appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Marquis Data Breach Affects 672,000 Individuals
item.description:
It was previously estimated that more than 1.6 million people may be affected by the Marquis data breach. The post Marquis Data Breach Affects 672,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran Readied Cyberattack Capabilities for Response Prior to Epic Fury
item.description:
Analysis reveals a six-month buildup of Iran-linked cyber infrastructure, including US-based shell companies, designed to weather kinetic strikes and ensure the resilience of its global hacking operations. The post Iran Readied Cyberattack Capabilities for Response Prior to Epic Fury appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys
item.description:
Latest ScreenConnect version adds encrypted storage and management to prevent unauthorized access to machine keys. The post Critical ScreenConnect Vulnerability Exposes Machine Keys appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management
item.description:
The company will invest in R&D, product expansion across AI frameworks, and in scaling go-to-market and sales efforts. The post Oasis Security Raises $120 Million for Agentic Access Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Interlock ransomware gang exploited Cisco firewall zero-day weeks before disclosure: Amazon
item.description:
The Interlock ransomware gang recently exploited a zero-day vulnerability in a popular line of Cisco firewalls before the bug was disclosed publicly, according to an Amazon report.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: White House pours cold water on cyber ‘letters of marque’ speculation
item.description:
The Trump administration is not considering cyber “letters of marque” or allowing private companies to carry out cyberattacks on behalf of the U.S. government, senior White House officials said this week, pushing back on growing speculation about the role of industry in U.S. cyber operations.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FBI, CISA warn on Microsoft Intune risks after Iran-linked cyberattack on Stryker
item.description:
The attackers behind a recent attack on Stryker did not use malware, instead breaking into a legitimate Microsoft device management system called Intune and wiping the company’s data that way.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New Android malware hiding in streaming apps to spy on users’ personal notes
item.description:
A newly discovered Android malware is masking itself within television streaming apps in order to steal users' passwords and banking data and spy on their personal notes, researchers have found.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Tecnocad Group
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to COINBASECARTEL have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: COINBASECARTEL | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach
item.description:
A data breach has been announced by Tieu Dental Corporation in California. The Children’s Council of San Francisco has determined […] The post California Dental Care Provider Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds
item.description:
Meadowlark Hills retirement community in Kansas and MedPeds Associates of Sarasota in Florida have announced data breaches. The Beast ransomware […] The post Ransomware Group Claims Attacks on Meadowlark Hills Retirement Community & MedPeds appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach
item.description:
The Chicago, IL-based Catholic health system CommonSpirit Health has announced that it has been affected by a security incident at […] The post CommonSpirit Health Patients Affected by Vendor Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been reported by Cedar Valley Services and Health Dimensions Group in Minnesota, and Community Nurse in […] The post PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been announced by Delta Medical Systems in Wisconsin, Ansell Healthcare Products in New Jersey, and FuturHealth […] The post Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE
item.description:
Trinity Health and the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center are notifying patients about potential unauthorized access to patient data by […] The post Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
item.description:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: CISA Advises U.S. Organziations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is urging U.S. organizations to strengthen administrative controls for the Intune endpoint […] The post CISA Advises U.S. Organziations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Marquis: Ransomware gang stole data of 672K people in cyberattack
item.description:
Marquis, a Texas-based financial services provider, revealed this week that a ransomware gang stole the data of over 670,000 individuals in an August 2025 cyberattack that also disrupted operations at 74 banks across the United States. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Ransomware gang exploits Cisco flaw in zero-day attacks since January
item.description:
The Interlock ransomware gang has been exploiting a maximum severity remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Cisco's Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) software in zero-day attacks since late January. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch Zimbra XSS flaw exploited in attacks
item.description:
CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to secure their servers against an actively exploited vulnerability in the Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Aura confirms data breach exposing 900,000 marketing contacts
item.description:
Identity protection company Aura has confirmed that an unauthorized party gained access to nearly 900,000 customer records containing names and email addresses. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Critical Microsoft SharePoint flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description:
A critical Microsoft SharePoint vulnerability patched in January is now being exploited in attacks, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA urges US orgs to secure Microsoft Intune systems after Stryker breach
item.description:
CISA warned U.S. organizations to follow Microsoft guidance to strengthen the Intune endpoint management tool after a cyberattack exploited it to wipe medical technology giant Stryker's systems. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
item.description:
Hackers part of APT28, a state-backed threat group linked to Russia's military intelligence service (GRU), are exploiting a Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) vulnerability in attacks targeting Ukrainian government entities. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: FBI seizes Handala data leak site after Stryker cyberattack
item.description:
The FBI has seized two websites used by the Handala hacktivist group after the threat actors conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker that wiped approximately 80,000 devices. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Bitrefill blames North Korean Lazarus group for cyberattack
item.description:
Crypto-powered gift card store Bitrefill says that the attack it suffered at the beginning of the month was likely perpetrated by North Korean hackers of the Bluenoroff group. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Navia discloses data breach impacting 2.7 million people
item.description:
Navia Benefit Solutions, Inc. (Navia) is informing nearly 2.7 million individuals of a data breach that exposed their sensitive information to attackers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: The “Internet YIFF Machine” leaks millions of “anonymous” tips to DDoSecrets
item.description:
Mikael Thalen reports: A California resident had an urgent message for the police. A family tied to Mexico’s notorious Sinaloa drug cartel was trafficking hundreds of pounds of marijuana at a time, and the tipster knew how the police could apprehend these “highly violent” people who “always carry weapons.” Equally urgent to the tipster: secrecy.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hackers can raid iOS 18 with an infected link. Have you updated yet to iOS26?
item.description:
Emma Roth reports: If you’ve been putting off an update to iOS 26, now might be the time to do it. On Wednesday, security researchers published findings on a new hacking tool that targets iPhones running iOS 18.4 to 18.6.2, as reported earlier by Wired. The “DarkSword” exploit allows bad actors to scoop up the personal information... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Chilling Effects: NZ GP stops uploading patient notes to MyIndici as a precaution
item.description:
Kate Green reports: A Wellington GP clinic has stopped uploading consultation notes to patient portal MyIndici as an extra layer of protection for patients’ data. MyIndici said it hasn’t been compromised, but there have been two high-profile security breaches involving health data at other companies in the past few months – ManageMyHealth, and then MediMap. Newlands Medical... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Personal information of 16,000 individuals leaked from Seoul National University Hospital
item.description:
Jung Si-Nae reports: Patient records of around 16,000 individuals were partially leaked from Seoul National University Hospital due to a clerical error. The hospital announced the data breach on its website on Thursday. A staff member mistakenly entered the wrong email address while sending an internal message at 2:07 p.m. on Saturday, resulting in patient... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyberattack leaves drivers with required breathalyzer test systems in 46 states unable to start vehicles
item.description:
Brad Rogers reports: A cyberattack has shut down a nationwide breathalyzer test system found in vehicles of OUI offenders, impacting thousands of drivers in Maine and 45 other states. Intoxalock is the company targeted by the attack. Once the device is installed, drivers have to pass a breathalyzer test before they can start their vehicle.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Deaconess patients’ sensitive data stolen in vendor breach
item.description:
Houston Harwood reports: A data breach at a third-party medical records vendor exposed the personal and health information of patients at two Deaconess Health System hospitals in Western Kentucky, the Evansville-based health system disclosed nearly two months after the breach itself occurred. The breach did not affect Deaconess’s internal computer systems or its electronic medical... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: Hackers part of APT28, a state-backed threat group linked to Russia’s military intelligence service (GRU), are exploiting a Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) vulnerability in attacks targeting Ukrainian government entities. This high-severity security flaw (tracked as CVE-2025-66376 and patched in early November) stems from a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that unauthenticated attackers can exploit to gain remote... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: UMMC continues investigating cyberattack and recovering from impact.
item.description:
In February, the University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) announced it was closing dozens of its clinics statewide after a cyberattack disrupted multiple IT systems and cut off access to its Epic EMR platform, which contained patients’ medical records. The attack resulted in a nine-day service suspension, during which most outpatient clinics statewide were closed,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Justice Department Disrupts Iranian Cyber Enabled Psychological Operations
item.description:
A Court-Authorized Domain Seizure Removes Four Websites Facilitating the Islamic Republic of Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security’s Hacking Efforts Tied to Psychological Operations and Transnational Repression WASHINGTON – Today, the Justice Department announced the seizure of four domains as part of an ongoing effort to disrupt hacking and transnational repression schemes conducted by the Islamic Republic... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: North Carolina tech worker found guilty of insider attack netting $2.5M ransom
item.description:
Matt Kapko reports: A 27-year-old North Carolina man was found guilty of six counts of extortion for a series of crimes he committed while working as a data analyst contractor for a D.C.-based international technology company, the Justice Department said Thursday. Cameron Nicholas Curry, also known as “Loot,” stole a trove of corporate data, including sensitive... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.description:
Gasteiger Design reported a ransomware. Attributed to DragonForce.
